chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Criminal Bail Application No.S-276 of 2022. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objections. For hearing of main case. 15.08.2022. Applicant is present on ad interim pre-arrest bail. Ms. Safa Hisbani, Assistant Prosecutor General. ORDER MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- Applicant, present, has been heard in person as his counsel is absent. This is a case of trespass and theft of Rs.3,000,000/- and gold ornaments from the spare parts shop of complainant situated in Shahbaz Colony Dadu on 12.06.2021 at 0220 hours. The FIR was registered however on 01.07.2021 on the basis of footage of CCTV Camera showing two accused entering shop and looting property. Subsequently, co- accused Bashir Ahmed and Kaleemullah were arrested, identified by the complainant party at police station to be the same as visible in CCTV footage and some of the looted property was also recovered from them. One of PWs namely Amir in his further statement dated 09.07.2021 has stated that he has come to know that applicant is also the culprit, however, neither the source of such information is disclosed nor the substance on the basis of which such statement is made is available in police record, which makes the case against applicant to be of further inquiry. Besides, as per learned APG all the material witnesses have been examined in this case and the trial is at the verge of conclusion. In the circumstances, this bail application is allowed and ad-interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicant vide order 21.03.2022 is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial court while deciding the case on merits.

In the circumstances, this bail application is allowed and ad-interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicant vide order 21.03.2022 is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial court while deciding the case on merits. JUDGE Irfan Ali
